{{Infobox character
| name = Doctor Nova
| appears_in = The Last Horizon
| titles = wanted fugitive, Aether Technician, Mell D-One-Twenty-Five, Archmage of sealing and binding, legendary criminal
| abilities = sealing and binding, deceive
| affiliations = Advocates
| introduced = The Engineer
}}

''See also: [[Doctor Nova Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Doctor Nova, also known as [[Mell D-One-Twenty-Five]], is a Master [[Aether Technician]] and the inventor of the [[Divine Titans]]. She is recognized by her signature creations, [[Nova-Bots]], which typically feature a single red light in the center of their visors. While she is considered a highly wanted fugitive by some, she has also been held in unlawful captivity. Her professional attire consists of an elaborate white coat with blue edges, a visor, and various [[Aethertech]] consoles and tools attached to her arms and belt. Beyond her work with [[Titans]], she is the creator of the android [[Starhammer]], though she has expressed concerns regarding his malfunctioning state and violent behavior. She is noted for her expertise in [[Aethertech]] and her interest in research regarding genetic engineering.}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Also known as [[Mell D-One-Twenty-Five]], Doctor Nova is an [[Aether Technician]] and [[Archmage of sealing and binding]] who created [[Starhammer]]. As the designer of [[Nova-Bots]] featuring a single red light in their visors, she was present during the initial activation of [[Starhammer]]'s [[Evolution Engine]]. Though once held in custody by the Advocates, she became a legendary criminal alongside [[Spellbinder]] and was eventually branded a wanted fugitive. After being targeted and kidnapped, her current location remains unknown, though she is recognized for her deceptive nature and her past connection to the narrator.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Doctor Nova]] is a character mentioned by [[Mell]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c6">''The Engineer'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] visited [[Doctor Nova]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c8">''The Engineer'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Doctor Nova]] is a wanted fugitive.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c11">''The Engineer'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The narrator was [[Doctor Nova]]’s partner in an alternate [[life]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c16">''The Engineer'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Doctor Nova]] has been kidnapped.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c31">''The Engineer'', chapter 31</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Doctor Nova]] is the creator of a specific design for [[Nova-Bots]] featuring a single red light in their visors.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c32">''The Engineer'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Doctor Nova]] was targeted by someone.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c33">''The Engineer'', chapter 33</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Doctor Nova]] is an [[Aether Technician]] who was held in custody by the Advocates.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c35">''The Engineer'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Doctor Nova]] is also known as [[Mell D-One-Twenty-Five]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c35" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Doctor Nova]] was present during the first activation of [[Starhammer]]'s [[Evolution Engine]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c40">''The Engineer'', chapter 40</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Doctor Nova]] is [[Mell]]'s voice and an [[Archmage of sealing and binding]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47">''The Engineer'', chapter 47</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Doctor Nova]] and [[Spellbinder]] were legendary criminals throughout the galaxy.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c49">''The Engineer'', chapter 49</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Doctor Nova]] is a person whose location is unknown.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c51">''The Engineer'', chapter 51</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Doctor Nova]] made [[Starhammer]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c52">''The Engineer'', chapter 52</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Doctor Nova]] is known for her ability to deceive.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c54">''The Engineer'', chapter 54</ref>}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Doctor Nova]] is a persona or title used by [[Mell]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c4">''The Knight'', chapter 4</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“The Advocates might not know who went after Doctor Nova,<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c33" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“The magic under your feet is a sealing spell,” came Doctor Nova’s voice. Mell’s voice.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{The Last Horizon nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon characters]]
[[Category:Advocates]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
